message:
perf(robot_overview): call single-purpose top_k_unlock_set instead of advanced_insights

build_robot_overview_output was calling analyzer.advanced_insights(),
which computes six analyses (top_k_set, coverage_set, k_paths,
cycle_break, parallel_cut, parallel_gain) — but only top_k_set is used
for the unlock_maximizers field. On large graphs, the other five
computations are each O(V·E) or worse and measurably delay every
--robot-overview / --robot-orient invocation for no benefit.

Changes:
- Add `Analyzer::top_k_unlock_set(k)` — a single-purpose helper that
  computes only the submodular unlock ranking.
- Add `compute_top_k_set_public` in advanced.rs so the previously
  private `compute_top_k_set` is reachable without refactoring the
  internal function signature.
- Use the new helper in build_robot_overview_output and in the #4
  regression test.

Empirical behavior unchanged: on stress_large_500.jsonl the orient
payload still surfaces SL-000 with marginal_unlocks=49 as the first
unlock_maximizer. Runtime reduced by the cost of 5 unused analyses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -445,6 +445,19 @@ impl Analyzer {
         advanced::compute_advanced_insights(&self.graph, &self.metrics)
     }
 
+    /// Compute ONLY the top-k submodular unlock-maximizing set.
+    ///
+    /// Prefer this over `advanced_insights()` when the caller needs just the
+    /// unlock ranking. `advanced_insights()` additionally computes coverage,
+    /// k-paths, cycle-break, parallel-cut, and parallel-gain — each scales
+    /// with graph size, and on a 5k-issue graph that's seconds of wasted work
+    /// when all you want is top_k_set. See issue #4 (`--robot-overview`) for
+    /// the motivating use case.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn top_k_unlock_set(&self, k: usize) -> advanced::TopKSetResult {
+        advanced::compute_top_k_set_public(&self.graph, &self.metrics, k)
+    }
+
     #[must_use]
     pub fn priority(
         &self,
